description: iOS learning app for autistic children covering early skills such as letters, colors, emotions, numbers, and vocabulary; listed in autism-app roundups and app-focused research as a skills-support tool.[1][2][4][5]
title: Autism iHelp  Alphabet, Colors, Emotions, Numbers, etc.

description: A specialized mood tracking mobile app for children with autism, using emoticons, colors, and simple language to facilitate emotional self-awareness and recording of feelings in an accessible, child-friendly interface.
title: Affect (Mood Tracking App for Autism)

description: A mobile app designed to help children with autism understand and communicate their feelings through images and animated characters, teaching them to recognize and label emotions in a visual, engaging format.
title: Autism Emotion App

description: A mobile app designed for families and clinicians to screen for autism in children at home. Uses video-based activities and machine learning to analyze social responses, with 77% sensitivity and 91% specificity in clinical validation studies.
title: ASDetect - Autism Screening App

description: Leading AAC app for iOS that helps children with limited verbal skills communicate using symbols, pictures, and text-to-speech voices.
title: Proloquo2Go

description: Comprehensive AAC app for iOS and Android offering wide vocabulary customization and text-to-speech for nonverbal communication.
title: TouchChat HD  AAC with WordPowerTM

description: Free AAC app for iOS and Android with no ads, designed for speech-impaired children to build sentences using pictures and text.
title: LetMeTalk

description: iOS version of the LetMeTalk app providing alternative communication tools for children with autism and speech delays.
title: LetMeTalk (iOS)

description: AI-powered AAC app for iOS and Android using advanced word prediction and text-to-speech for nonverbal autism and aphasia.
title: Spoken - Tap to Talk AAC

description: iOS app for creating customizable choice boards to support communication in individuals with autism and other developmental disabilities.
title: ChoiceBoard Maker

description: A visual scheduling app that uses images, timers, and checklists to create daily routines and reminders, helping young adults with autism manage time and transitions effectively.
title: Choiceworks - Visual Schedule, Timer & Reminders for Autism

description: iOS app designed to help individuals with autism regulate emotions through guided breathing, calming visuals, and soothing soundscapes; also available on Android via Google Play.
title: Peaceable - Calming & Relaxation App

description: Free iOS and Android app created by Teen Health to teach CBT-based tools for anxiety management, including grounding exercises and mindfulness techniques for neurodivergent users.
title: MindShift CBT - Anxiety Relief App

description: Non-profit meditation app for iOS and Android offering age-specific mindfulness programs, including 'Youth' and 'Children' modules designed to support emotional regulation in autistic individuals.
title: Smiling Mind - Meditation & Calm

description: iOS and Android haptic feedback app providing tapping and sliding interactions for sensory regulation; ideal for environments where physical fidget tools are not available.
title: Tappy Fidget - Sensory Regulation Tool

description: iOS app designed by child psychologist Jacqueline Vorpahl to teach children with autism specific relaxation techniques through animated stories and guided mindfulness exercises.
title: Chill Outz - Relaxation for Kids

description: Official product page for the Zones of Regulation app (iOS/Android), based on a framework to help children consciously regulate behaviors, emotions, and alertness levels to improve self-regulation.
title: Zones of Regulation App

description: A subscription-based AAC app by Tobii Dynavox featuring symbol-based communication and a free trial period for unlocking full speech capabilities.
title: TD Snap

description: Community-curated directory of AAC tools, apps, and providers with a focus on autism support and language development.
title: PrAACtical AAC Directory

description: A paid AAC app for nonverbal individuals, including those with autism, to serve as a daily communication tool and build language skills. Available on Apple/iOS.
title: Proloquo2Go - Augmentative and Alternative Communication App

description: Official product page on the Apple App Store for Proloquo2Go, featuring app details, pricing, and user reviews for assistive communication.
title: Proloquo2Go on Apple App Store

description: An iOS app helping autistic children grasp 'first this, then that' concepts to manage transitions and daily routines.
title: First Then Visual Schedule HD - Visual Scheduling App

description: An app designed to assist autistic students with daily routines, emotional regulation, and waiting skills through interactive visual tools.
title: Choiceworks - Daily Routines and Emotional Regulation

description: Official documentation and support resources for Proloquo2Go, including setup guides, training materials, and usage tips for autism support.
title: Proloquo2Go Documentation - ATIS

description: A free Apple app for early elementary students with autism to learn letter identification and phonics through engaging tracing and sound activities.
title: ABC Kids  Tracing & Phonics - Free Learning App

description: Official repository for Proloquo2Go development updates, open-source components, and community contributions for assistive communication tools.
title: Proloquo2Go GitHub Repository - ATIS

description: Open-source RFID-based communication tool enabling independent verbal picture communication for nonverbal autistic users, with support for custom symbol mapping and voice output.
title: ProxTalker

description: Open-source toolkit for building and customizing AAC applications for autistic individuals, including symbol libraries, voice output modules, and user interface templates.
title: AAC for Autism Toolkit

description: Popular AAC app with open-source backend components, enabling autistic users to communicate via text-to-speech, with customizable symbol sets and voice options.
title: Speech Assistant AAC

description: Official Microsoft To Do page offering a free task manager with visual schedules, recurring task support, and integration with Microsoft 365 for managing daily routines and startup goals.
title: Microsoft To Do - Task Manager App

description: A browser-based Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) system with text-to-speech, designed to enhance environmental intelligibility for autistic individuals.
title: cboard - AAC System

description: University of Michigan open-source project combining features of common scheduling systems to help youth with disabilities develop accountability and independence in daily routines.
title: Open-source software for scheduling independence

description: A community dedicated to developing open-source applications specifically for people diagnosed with autism and other impairments.
title: Open Autism

description: An iPad app by Good Karma Applications that helps children with autism understand daily routines through visual scheduling.
title: First-Then Visual Schedule HD

description: An open-source font with evenly spaced, italicized letters designed to improve reading direction clarity for people with dyslexia.
title: OpenDyslexic Font

description: Open-source code repository for Tiimo app, providing accessible tools for visual timelines and executive function support in neurodivergent scheduling.
title: Tiimo GitHub Repository

description: An open-source, caregiver-mediated mobile app that prompts parents to log challenging behaviors and suggests individualized, function-based interventions tailored to their child with autism.
title: Family Behavior Support App (Open Source)

description: An open-source, AI-driven communication app for minimally verbal children with autism, allowing parents to customize communication boards and track development milestones.
title: AACessTalk (Open Source Communication App)

description: An open-source repository of caregiver-implemented intervention materials based on the Early Start Denver Model, designed for parents to support social interaction in young children with autism.
title: Early Start Denver Model (Open Intervention Repository)

description: A community-driven open-source toolkit for autism parents, including templates for care plans, advocacy guides, and milestone trackers, freely available for modification and distribution.
title: Autism Parent Support Open Source Toolkit (ASPT)

description: Open-source resources for siblings of individuals with autism, including guides and support group materials that parents can use to foster family understanding and connection.
title: Sibling Support Project Open Resources

description: A wiki-based, open-source guide for parents of children with autism, covering topics from diagnosis to therapy options, with community-reviewed content and downloadable PDFs.
title: Open Autism Parent Guide (OAPG)

description: Open-source tools for implementing Naturalistic Developmental Behavioral Interventions (NDBIs) at home, including video tutorials and interactive checklists for parents supporting autism development.
title: NDBI Self-Help Tools (Open Source)

description: AAC speech communication app for non-verbal children and adults, enabling neurodivergent users to express themselves through symbols, text, and audio.
title: JABtalk

description: Official Apple App Store listing for Be My Eyes, providing download and feature details for the live video assistance app supporting blind, low-vision, and neurodivergent users.
title: Be My Eyes App Store Page

description: An initiative dedicated to developing open-source applications for people diagnosed with autism and other impairments, providing adaptive digital tools for neurodivergent support.
title: Open Autism Tools
